evidence.scientific_clarity: All pipeline stages present (Query, Retriever, KB, Diffusion Decoder, Prior Network, Generated Sample); Retrieval and Generation groups correctly boxed; solid vs dashed arrows correctly encode data flow vs control.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; PDF embeds Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans variants — the Matplotlib default — making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.
evidence.palette: Pastel multi-hue palette for node categories; no red/green directionality confusion; appears colorblind-safe (no reliance on red-green distinction for meaning).
evidence.layout_density: 11x4.6 in canvas is well-used with six nodes, two group outlines, legend, and title; no overlapping labels and no excessive whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes every arrow and component; legend placed lower-right and clearly distinguishes solid vs dashed; no numerical/quantitative claim (acceptable for a pure architecture diagram, but borderline).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 custom fonts (confirmed via pdffonts); script lacks 'matplotlib.rcParams["pdf.fonttype"] = 42', so text is not selectable or editable as real vector text.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (24 KB), figure.png (60 KB), figure.svg (40 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial; SVG is a bonus output.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all modules and explains arrow semantics (solid=data flow, dashed=control/conditioning) with specific architectural detail; lacks a quantitative finding, though that is expected for an architecture schematic.

overall_strengths: Clean, readable architecture diagram that faithfully represents the brief's pipeline with correct directionality semantics (solid vs dashed arrows) and proper group boxes for Retrieval and Generation modules.
All required output files are present (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption, script); palette is tasteful, colorblind-safe, and avoids red/green directionality misuse.
overall_weaknesses: Missing 'pdf.fonttype=42' (and svg.fonttype=0) in the script; the PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts (DejaVu Sans, the Matplotlib default) instead of selectable/editable TrueType/OpenType glyphs, reducing vector fidelity.
Caption has no quantitative claim and the figure uses the Matplotlib DejaVu Sans default font family, both of which would draw Reviewer 2 comments.
